            "text": "/** Search tab: default (&#96;useSearch&#96; + S4) vs results State 2 (S5b) + Detail on Search stack. */"

            "text": "export function SearchScreen({ navigation }: Props): JSX.Element {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "  const insets = useSafeAreaInsets();"

          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "  const selectedGenreIndex = useMemo(() =\u003e {"
          },

            "text": "    const n = normalizeSearchTerm(query);"
          },

            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "    if (n.length === 0) {"

          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "      return 0;"

          {
            "lineNumber": 52,

          {

          {

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60
          },

            "lineNumber": 61,

          {

            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "/**"
          },

            "text": " * Shape-matched skeleton for Search results — count line + 2-column poster grid (PSD-Search §4, §7 S6)."
          },

            "text": " */"

            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },

            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "const SKELETON_ROW_COUNT = 3;"

          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "function ResultCardSkeleton({ colWidth }: { colWidth: number }): JSX.Element {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "      \u003cShimmerBox style={[styles.poster, { height: posterHeight }]} /\u003e"

            "text": "      \u003cShimmerBox style={styles.titleLine} /\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "      \u003cShimmerBox style={styles.yearLine} /\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    \u003c/View\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "  );"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "export function SearchResultsGridSkeleton(): JSX.Element {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "  const { width: windowWidth } = useWindowDimensions();"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Here is what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
The Search “Trending” grid (and the results grid) set each column width as if the gutter were **`spacing.md`**, but the row actually used a larger **`gap`** (`spacing.xl` on the default view, `spacing.xxl` on results). So **two cards + gap were wider than the row**, and with `flexWrap: 'wrap'` the second card moved to the next line. You got one narrow card per row and empty space on the right.

### Fix
Use one **`gridRowGap`** value for both:
- the **`gap`** on the row, and  
- the **`gridColWidth`** formula: `(windowWidth - horizontalPadding * 2 - gridRowGap) / 2`

### Files updated
1. **`src/components/search/SearchDefaultView.tsx`** — `gridRowGap = spacing.xl`, width math and row `gap` aligned (matches your Search tab + genre line UI).
2. **`src/components/search/SearchResultsStateView.tsx`** — `gridRowGap = spacing.xxl`, same alignment for search results.
3. **`src/components/search/SearchTrendingSkeleton.tsx`** — same width/gap alignment as the trending grid so the skeleton does not drift.

`SearchResultsGridSkeleton.tsx` was already using **`md`** for both width and row gap, so it was left as is.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes. After a reload, you should see **two posters per row** on Search (trending and results).

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Yes. **`SeeAllScreen`** imports and renders the shared **`ContentCard`** from `src/components/common/ContentCard`.

It is wired in `renderItem` for the two-column `FlatList`:

```17:18:src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx
import { ContentCard } from '../components/common/ContentCard';
import { SeeAllGridSkeleton } from '../components/seeAll/SeeAllGridSkeleton';
```

```65:77:src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx
  const renderItem = useCallback(
    ({ item }: { item: TmdbMovieListItem }) => (
      <ContentCard
        onPress={() => {
          onOpenDetail(item.id);
        }}
        posterPath={item.poster_path}
        rating={item.vote_average}
        style={{ width: gridColWidth }}
        subtitle={formatListMovieSubtitle(item)}
        title={item.title}
      />
    ),
    [gridColWidth, onOpenDetail],
  );
```

The file comment also states the screen is a 2-column **`ContentCard`** grid. Loading uses **`SeeAllGridSkeleton`** from `src/components/seeAll/`, not `ContentCard`, but the list cells are `ContentCard`.

**Polishing watchlist screen header**

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Here is what was updated:

### What changed

1. **`src/components/watchlist/WatchlistEmptyState.tsx`**
   - Matches **`watchlist-empty.html` / `watchlist_empty.png`**: empty block is in a **`centerSlot`** with `flexGrow: 1` and `justifyContent: 'center'` so the bookmark, copy, and CTA sit in the middle when there is extra height.
   - **Bookmark disc**: `surface` tint + **`outline_variant`** 1px ring (same idea as the HTML `ring-outline-variant/10`).
   - **Headline**: `headline-rail` (~24px) for the “Your watchlist is empty” line, closer to the HTML `text-2xl`.
   - **Popular Recommendations**: no more ghost-only row at 20% opacity. While **`popularRecommendations.loading`**, it still uses **`GhostPosterPlaceholderGrid`** (4 tiles). When data exists, it shows up to **4** trending movies in a **2×2** grid using shared **`ContentCard`** (posters, ratings, **`formatListMovieSubtitle`**), fully opaque and **tappable** via new prop **`onPressRecommendation`**.
   - Section title uses **wider tracking** (`2.4`, ~`0.2em` on 12px) like the reference.

2. **`src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx`**
   - Empty watchlist **`ScrollView`** content uses a single inner column with **`flexGrow: 1`** so the empty state can expand and center.
   - Passes **`onPressRecommendation`** → **`navigation.navigate('Detail', { movieId })`**.
   - **`styles.emptyEditorialHeader`** on the header for **`marginBottom: spacing.xxxl`** (~`mb-10` in the HTML).

3. **`src/components/watchlist/WatchlistScreenHeader.tsx`**
   - Optional **`style`** prop merged after **`headerBlock`** so the empty screen can add extra editorial spacing without a wrapper.

### Why
The design reference shows a centered empty state, then a real **Popular Recommendations** grid. The hook already loads **trending week** when the watchlist is empty (`useWatchlist`); the UI was still rendering only muted placeholders. It now uses that **`popularRecommendations.data`** with **`ContentCard`** for parity with **See All** / home cards.

### Verification
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean  
- `npm test` — all **43** tests passed
